title: SADC Secretariat Internship Programme
description: The Southern African Development Community (SADC) Secretariat, headquartered in Gaborone, Botswana, runs a six-month internship program placing university students and recent graduates in its various directorates and units to gain practical policy and administrative experience. SADC pays interns a monthly living allowance of US$250, while the intern's home Member State government covers relocation, insurance, and related travel costs.
visaNote: Interns are placed at the SADC Secretariat HQ in Gaborone; the intern's Member State government is responsible for arranging travel and any necessary documentation.

title: Mauritius Oceanography Institute Research Internship
description: The Mauritius Oceanography Institute (MOI), a Mauritian government research body based in Albion, hosts student and graduate interns in its physical oceanography, marine chemistry, biology, marine geosciences, and biotechnology units on a first-come, first-served rolling basis. The internship is an unpaid research placement providing lab and field access and supervision by MOI scientists; applicants apply directly by emailing a CV to the Institute's Director.
visaNote: No formal work visa is issued for this unpaid research placement; non-citizen applicants should confirm entry/residence requirements with Mauritian immigration authorities before arrival.

title: ACOR Amman Prize Fellowship for Postgraduate Research in Jordan
description: The American Center of Research (ACOR) in Amman awards the Amman Prize to support four months of research residency in Jordan for a scholar who has completed their formal course of study, in any field of scientific, social-scientific, or humanistic research concerned with Jordan. The award is $25,000, including $5,000 for travel and research expenses, plus room, board, and a private workspace at ACOR's Amman facility during the residency.
visaNote: Fellows enter Jordan on a research/residency basis coordinated with ACOR; not a formal labor work visa.

title: Orient-Institut Beirut Postdoctoral Fellowship
description: The Orient-Institut Beirut (OIB), a German academic research institute of the Max Weber Foundation, awards residential postdoctoral fellowships of up to 10 months (beginning around September) to scholars with a completed doctorate pursuing humanities or social-science research projects that benefit from an extended stay in Lebanon. Fellows receive a monthly stipend of 1,900 EUR plus a one-time 600 EUR travel allowance for those arriving from abroad, and depending on availability, office space at the Institute in Beirut.
visaNote: Fellows arrange a research-residency stay in Lebanon in coordination with the Institute; this is not a standard labor work visa.

title: SALAM Summer Arabic Language and Media Study Abroad Scholarship
description: The Sultan Qaboos Cultural Center (SQCC), the U.S.-based cultural office representing the Sultanate of Oman, sponsors the Summer Arabic Language and Media (SALAM) Scholarship for American undergraduate and graduate students to complete an intensive summer Arabic-language and media program hosted at the Sultan Qaboos Institute in Manah, Oman. The fully-funded scholarship covers international airfare between Washington, D.C. and Muscat, tuition, housing, three daily meals, weekend excursions, internet, and program activity fees.
visaNote: SQCC/the host institute coordinate entry arrangements for accepted students; this is a study program, not employment.

title: Al Jazeera Media Institute Fellowship Programme
description: The Al Jazeera Media Institute (AJMI) in Doha runs an annual three-month fellowship for journalists and media researchers (bachelor's degree plus at least two years of media experience) to research a journalism/media topic and produce a roughly 7,000-word paper, with Phase 1 conducted remotely and Phase 2 in person at Al Jazeera Media Network headquarters in Doha. The fellowship grant covers travel and accommodation for the in-person phase in Qatar.
visaNote: AJMI/Al Jazeera Media Network coordinate entry logistics for the in-person residency phase in Doha for accepted fellows.
